title: Acetosella arachnoidea
taxonomy: Acetosella arachnoidea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It belongs to the genus Acetosella, which is a part of the larger plant family Oxalidaceae. The Oxalidaceae family consists of about 700 species, which are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world, particularly in Asia and Africa. Acetosella arachnoidea is also known as the 'Coral Creeper' or 'Coral Saxifrage'.
uses: Acetosella arachnoidea, also known as the "spider clover," has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Acetosella arachnoidea, also known as spiderwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Oxalidaceae, characterized by its unique, hairy leaves and white or pink flowers. The leaves of Acetosella arachnoidea have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Acetosella arachnoidea is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin issues and wound healing.
